objective.subject: English
objective.statement: Attend to short spoken stories, poems, instructions and information, retrieve explicit details, order key events and make simple inferences from stated clues.
misconceptions: Chooses a picture because it matches one familiar word but not the whole spoken meaning.
Remembers a detail but confuses what happened first, next or last.
Guesses from the picture or prior knowledge instead of using a clue from the audio.
Thinks needing a replay means the answer must change or that listening quickly is the goal.
objective.parent_explanation: Can listen to short stories and information, remember important details, put events in order and use a heard clue to work something out.
objective.teacher_evidence: Records retrieval, sequence, vocabulary, inference and clue selection separately from replay use, expressive speech, decoding, working-memory support and motor response mode.

activities.title: I can listen to a short text, remember important parts and show what it means.
activities.prompt: Listen for three treasure tokens: who, what happened and where.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Choose your listening plan: still body, quiet hands, eyes where they feel comfortable, replay ready.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Create an inclusive attention routine without demanding eye contact or stillness as proof of listening.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Get comfortable. You may look at the listening card, look away, move quietly or use replay.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: A choice board shows comfortable listening options and a large replay button.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen for three treasure tokens: who, what happened and where.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Focus attention on a small, predictable set of explicit details.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: You do not need to remember every word. Listen for who, what and where.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three empty picture-token spaces appear before the audio begins.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen: 'Mina put on her red boots and splashed through the puddle by the gate.'
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Model retrieval from one complete spoken sentence without keyword matching.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Who? Mina. What? She splashed. Where? By the gate. Red tells us about the boots, but it is not the place.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Mina, splashing and gate pictures fill the three detail-token spaces after listening.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Listen once, pause to think, then tap or point to the picture that matches the whole meaning.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Practise retrieval and selection while resisting single-word picture distractors.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Hear the whole part before choosing. Replay if you need it. Check that every part of the picture fits.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three large picture choices appear only after the short audio finishes.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Tari takes an umbrella and the path is shiny. What clue suggests it has rained?
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Distinguish a simple text-based inference from guessing or picture knowledge.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Point to the part you heard that helped you work it out.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Umbrella and shiny-path clue cards connect to a rain picture.

teaching_sequence.child_prompt: Show Nixi what happened first, next and last, then tell or point to one clue.
teaching_sequence.learning_purpose: Gather sequencing and explanation evidence through speech or accessible non-verbal response.
teaching_sequence.audio_script: Put the three pictures in order. You can tell me, point, tap or ask an adult to say your answer.
teaching_sequence.visual_model: Three large numbered spaces and a clue token remain on screen.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Use your ears and attention to notice the spoken words.
vocabulary.audio_script: Listen means get ready to notice the important spoken words.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A calm ear icon beside a pause-and-ready card.

vocabulary.child_explanation: A small important part we hear, such as who, what or where.
vocabulary.audio_script: A detail is one important part of what you heard.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Three picture tokens labelled who, what and where.

vocabulary.child_explanation: The order things happen: first, next and last.
vocabulary.audio_script: Sequence means the order in which things happen.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: Three large stepping stones labelled first, next and last.

vocabulary.child_explanation: Something we hear that helps us work out an answer.
vocabulary.audio_script: A clue is a part you heard that helps you work something out.
vocabulary.visual_anchor: A spoken-word bubble pointing to a thinking picture.

manipulative.purpose: Answer explicit and inferential listening questions using large picture choices after a short audio text.
manipulative.interaction_notes: Audio plays before choices appear; learners tap, keyboard-select, switch-scan, point for an adult or use partner-assisted scanning. Replay count never reduces score.
manipulative.accessibility_notes: Produced human-listened audio only; no browser TTS; large targets; visible playback state; captions available to adults but hidden by default for the child task; volume control; and no microphone or eye-contact requirement.

adaptive_support.low_sensory: Use a quiet Wonder Garden listening nook, plain backgrounds, no music beneath speech and gentle confirmations without surprise sounds.
adaptive_support.reduced_motion: Replace moving story elements with static pictures that appear after audio and simple outlines around the selected response.
adaptive_support.audio_first: Use produced, human-listened UK narration with whole-text and sentence replay. Browser TTS is prohibited and unavailable audio must show an honest not-ready state.
adaptive_support.reading_support: Keep child print optional, use picture choices with spoken labels and show adult transcripts only outside the assessed listening view.
adaptive_support.attention_support: Use one short audio text per screen, a visible listen-think-show routine and choices that appear only after listening.
adaptive_support.processing_support: Allow unlimited replay, longer pauses, sentence-by-sentence mode, reduced two-choice sets and adult-controlled playback.
adaptive_support.confidence_support: Do not score replay count or response speed; accept pointing, tapping, AAC and adult-observed answers without recording the child's voice.
adaptive_support.eal_support: Check key story vocabulary and cultural context before assessment, use clear natural narration and avoid treating accent, spoken grammar or unfamiliar background knowledge as comprehension failure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retrieve one explicit person, action, object or place from a short spoken sentence or two-sentence text.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 5-15 second produced audio + delayed 2-4 picture choices + one whole-meaning distractor + replay
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Pictures must not reveal the answer before listening; distractors may share one word but must remain clearly wrong for the complete meaning.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Order two or three central events from a short spoken narrative, instruction or process.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 10-25 second produced audio + 2-3 event pictures + numbered spaces + whole/sentence replay
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Events must have an unambiguous order in the audio; picture descriptions must not leak first, next or last.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Use surrounding spoken words, tone-neutral narration and pictures to understand an age-appropriate word or phrase.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: short sentence pair + target word + 3 picture/meaning choices + replay + example/non-example check
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Do not assess prosody unless it is explicitly taught and human reviewed; vocabulary should be useful and inferable rather than obscure.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Make a simple inference and identify the spoken clue that supports it.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: 10-20 second text + inference picture choices + selectable clue tokens + speech/AAC/adult-scribed explanation
variant_blueprints.review_notes: The inference must be strongly supported and culturally accessible; accept more than one response only where each has a clear heard clue.

variant_blueprints.purpose: Retain listening routines across spaced review using mixed story, poem, instruction and information snippets.
variant_blueprints.generation_pattern: review queue + produced audio + optional adult transcript + one retrieval/sequence/inference response + replay evidence
variant_blueprints.review_notes: Replay remains unlimited and unpenalised; child-facing print must not turn the task into a decoding assessment.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Chooses a picture because it matches one familiar word but not the whole spoken meaning.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The selected picture includes one mentioned object but conflicts with the action, person or place.
repair_sequence: Place who, what and where tokens as each detail is heard.
Check every part of the chosen picture against the tokens.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner selects a whole-meaning match in a new one-sentence item.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Remembers a detail but confuses what happened first, next or last.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: All event pictures are recognised but placed in the wrong order.
repair_sequence: Replay one sentence or event at a time.
Place each picture immediately on a numbered path.
Retell by pointing from first to last.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner orders a fresh two- or three-event audio sequence.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Guesses from the picture or prior knowledge instead of using a clue from the audio.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner chooses a plausible answer but cannot select any supporting spoken detail.
repair_sequence: Hide answer pictures during the first replay.
Reveal choices and connect the heard clue to the answer.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner chooses an answer and its supporting clue in a new item.

misconception_repairs.misconception: Thinks needing a replay means the answer must change or that listening quickly is the goal.
misconception_repairs.detect_with: The learner avoids replay, rushes a response or changes a supported answer only because audio was replayed.
repair_sequence: Model replay as a normal expert listening tool.
Keep the current answer visible while replaying.
Change the answer only when a newly noticed detail provides evidence.
misconception_repairs.evidence_of_repair: The learner uses replay calmly and retains or revises an answer for an evidence-based reason.

feedback.companion_prompt: Show Nixi what happened first, next and last, then tell or point to one clue.
feedback.parent_summary: Can listen to short stories and information, remember important details, put events in order and use a heard clue to work something out.
feedback.teacher_evidence: Records retrieval, sequence, vocabulary, inference and clue selection separately from replay use, expressive speech, decoding, working-memory support and motor response mode.
next_actions: Route one-word picture matching to who-what-where checking.
Route sequence errors to sentence-by-sentence picture placement.
Route unsupported guesses to heard-clue selection.
Schedule mixed story, poem, instruction and information listening at 1, 3, 7, 14 and 30 days.

body.prompt: Listen, then choose where Mina splashed.
body.narration_script: Mina put on her red boots and splashed through the puddle by the gate.

hints: Listen for the place at the end.
Check the whole sentence, not only the word boots.
questions.explanation: The spoken sentence says Mina splashed through the puddle by the gate.

body.prompt: Listen, then put the pictures in order.
body.narration_script: First, Arun filled the watering can. Next, he watered the small sunflower. Last, he put the can beside the shed.
event_cards: put the can beside the shed

value: put the can beside the shed

hints: Replay and listen for first, next and last.
Find what Arun did before he watered the flower.
questions.explanation: Arun filled the can first, watered the sunflower next and put the can away last.

body.prompt: Listen. Which picture shows what peeked means here?
body.narration_script: Lulu peeked around the curtain. She showed only her eyes because she was not ready to come out.
choices: looking quickly from behind the curtain

expected_answer.value: looking quickly from behind the curtain

hints: The next sentence tells you what Lulu showed.
Which picture fits showing only her eyes?
questions.explanation: The clue that Lulu showed only her eyes helps us understand that she looked from behind the curtain.

body.prompt: Listen. Choose what probably happened, then choose the clue you heard.
body.narration_script: Tari carried an umbrella. Drops slid from the leaves, and the path shone under the morning light.
answer_choices: The path was made of gold
clue_choices: umbrella, drops and a shiny path

expected_answer.support: umbrella, drops and a shiny path

hints: What can make leaves drip and paths shine?
Choose the part you heard that supports your answer.
questions.explanation: The umbrella, drops and shiny path are spoken clues that it had probably rained.

reward_payload.explanation: Can listen to short stories and information, remember important details, put events in order and use a heard clue to work something out.
reward_payload.companion_prompt: Show Nixi what happened first, next and last, then tell or point to one clue.
